citations.narrative:
A wooden steps-and-platform structure for "Wilma" the bobcat is heavily weathered, cracking and exhibiting an uneven
surface. The structure is wobbly when touched. Unstable climbing surfaces for animals may pose a risk for falls or other
injuries. The outdoor housing facilities shall be structurally sound and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the
animals from injury.
Correct by 19 March 2026.

In the pantry where food for quail is stored, there is cleaning detergents and other substances toxic to birds present on
shelves above the dry food. Substances such as cleaning supplies and chemicals that are harmful to the birds but that are
required for normal husbandry practices must not be stored in food storage and preparation areas but may be stored in
cabinets.
Correct by 27 February 2026.

citations.desc: Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being.
citations.narrative:
In November of 2025 the companion of the Gambel's Quail passed away, leaving the current quail "Jasper" (a social
species) housed alone and unable to see and hear birds of their own or compatible species. The facility does not have a
documented special consideration to provide special attention regarding the enhancement to the environment for this
individual bird in accordance with the instructions of the attending veterinarian, or an approved exemption. The
environmental enhancement plan to promote the psychological well-being of birds requires special considerations for
individually housed birds to address the social needs of species of birds known to exist in social groups in nature, in
accordance with currently accepted professional standards.
Correct by 19 March 2026.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The aviary exhibit area for raptors is not kept clean or in good repair in order to protect the birds from injury.
***During the inspection, a bottle of dish soap detergent, a scrub brush, and water hose were observed stored within the
aviary that the Harris's Hawks were currently using. These materials were accessible to the animals and presented
potential risks, including exposure to toxic substances, ingestion of foreign materials, and physical injury from equipment.
The presence of improperly stored cleaning agents and maintenance items in the primary animal area demonstrates a
failure to provide safe and properly maintained facilities as required by the Animal Welfare Act.
***There are overgrown weeds throughout the interior of the aviary and along the exterior of the aviary walls. Some weeds
in the northeastern corner of the aviary appear a couple of feet tall. A water dish placed on the ground is obscured by
weeds. Failure to control weeds and grasses may attract pests that can introduce disease to the birds or cause damage
to the housing facilities.
Housing areas must be maintained in a manner that protects animals from hazards and ensures their safety and well-
being. Weeds, grasses, and bushes must be controlled so as to facilitate cleaning of the premises and pest control, and to
protect the health and wellbeing of the birds.
Correct from this date forward of 20 February 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
n

narrative:
3.125(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
A wooden steps-and-platform structure for "Wilma" the bobcat is heavily weathered, cracking and exhibiting an uneven 
surface. The structure is wobbly when touched. Unstable climbing surfaces for animals may pose a risk for falls or other 
injuries. The outdoor housing facilities shall be structurally sound and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the 
animals from injury.  
 
Correct by 19 March 2026. 
3.150(e) 
   
Facilities, general. 
In the pantry where food for quail is stored, there is cleaning detergents and other substances toxic to birds present on 
shelves above the dry food. Substances such as cleaning supplies and chemicals that are harmful to the birds but that are 
required for normal husbandry practices must not be stored in food storage and preparation areas but may be stored in 
cabinets. 
 
Correct by 27 February 2026. 
3.154(c)(4) 
   
Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being. 
In November of 2025 the companion of the Gambel's Quail passed away, leaving the current quail "Jasper" (a social 
species) housed alone and unable to see and hear birds of their own or compatible species. The facility does not have a 
documented special consideration to provide special attention regarding the enhancement to the environment for this 
individual bird in accordance with the instructions of the attending veterinarian, or an approved exemption. The 
environmental enhancement plan to promote the psychological well-being of birds requires special considerations for 
individually housed birds to address the social needs of species of birds known to exist in social groups in nature, in 
accordance with currently accepted professional standards.

Correct by 19 March 2026. 
3.158(c) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The aviary exhibit area for raptors is not kept clean or in good repair in order to protect the birds from injury.  
 
***During the inspection, a bottle of dish soap detergent, a scrub brush, and water hose were observed stored within the 
aviary that the Harris's Hawks were currently using. These materials were accessible to the animals and presented 
potential risks, including exposure to toxic substances, ingestion of foreign materials, and physical injury from equipment. 
The presence of improperly stored cleaning agents and maintenance items in the primary animal area demonstrates a 
failure to provide safe and properly maintained facilities as required by the Animal Welfare Act.  
 
***There are overgrown weeds throughout the interior of the aviary and along the exterior of the aviary walls. Some weeds 
in the northeastern corner of the aviary appear a couple of feet tall. A water dish placed on the ground is obscured by 
weeds. Failure to control weeds and grasses may attract pests that can introduce disease to the birds or cause damage 
to the housing facilities. 
 
Housing areas must be maintained in a manner that protects animals from hazards and ensures their safety and well-
being. Weeds, grasses, and bushes must be controlled so as to facilitate cleaning of the premises and pest control, and to 
protect the health and wellbeing of the birds. 
 
Correct from this date forward of 20 February 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
